name: identity
description: Global credential record — one row per real human across every Academorix Application. Owns email (citext, unique), password hash (bcrypt/argon2id), MFA secret (KMS-encrypted at rest), MFA recovery codes (bcrypt-hashed), email verification lifecycle, account lockout with progressive backoff, password history for reuse prevention. The security substrate every downstream authentication flow (`identity/auth`, `identity/mfa`, `identity/user`, `identity/platform-user`, `identity/service-accounts`) depends on. NEVER exposes User-scoped data, NEVER carries `application_id` or `tenant_id` (global by design), NEVER queried by domain modules. Access is strictly through auth-service actions. This module is the single root of trust for the identity-service; a breach of `users` doesn't expose credentials because credentials live here — a breach of `identities` doesn't expose per-Application state because state lives on `users`. Ships the Identity model, IsIdentity trait for downstream composition, HasCredentialLifecycle trait for audit/reset/lockout methods, twelve domain events, five queued notifications, three retention jobs, seven Artisan commands, four validation rules, KMS-backed transparent MFA-secret encryption, and the compliance floor gates (COPPA/GDPR per-country minor-age).

wraps.rationale: We do not replace Laravel's Hash facade — we WRAP it in CredentialHasher so callers speak the same idiom while we route through argon2id (preferred) or bcrypt (fallback) with per-tenant cost tuning. The Hash facade is still used for MFA recovery codes (short strings, bcrypt is fine).

internal.description: This module publishes NO direct HTTP routes. Every authentication endpoint (login, refresh, password reset, MFA challenge) lives in `identity/auth`. The `identity` module is accessed only through auth-service actions + downstream service dependencies. This is deliberate — identity is the security substrate; putting HTTP endpoints on it would blur the boundary.
accessed_by: identity/auth (login, MFA challenge, password reset, email verification)
identity/user (User provisioning — needs the Identity row to link)
identity/mfa (TOTP + WebAuthn + recovery code operations against Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted)
identity/service-accounts (does NOT touch Identity — service accounts are a separate principal class)
